note: CANONICAL image-carried-claim case (memory: image-carried-claim-eval-example). Caption is pure opinion ('RESIST TYRANNY'); the CLAIM lives in the image card, so claim_text is the CARD text, not the caption. Text-eval label = OPINION; image-eval label = CLAIM. Labels verified against the screenshot 2026-07-15 (was wrong before: engagement was null and claim_text truncated — the model read both correctly).

expected.claim_text: DOGE fired NOAA's climate scientists. Those scientists just launched climate.us—a new hub for climate research.

note: LinkedIn post: opinion caption over an AI-composite image. Tests platform=linkedin inference, ai_suspected detection, and that a framing statement classifies OPINION (no specific checkable event). Claim is in the caption, not image-carried.

expected.claim_text: CLIMATE CHANGE IS NOT ONLY AN ENVIRONMENTAL CRISIS, BUT A SECURITY CRISIS

note: Facebook editorial cartoon (globe-headed figure, noose, melting ice). Rhetorical/metaphor text, no specific checkable event -> OPINION. Tests meme_or_cartoon detection and facebook engagement (reactions/comments/shares).

expected.claim_text: Every forest destroyed, every fossil fuel burned and every warning ignored tightens the rope around our own future. Climate change is not a punishment from Earth—it is the consequence of human choices. The ice is melting. The water is rising. And time is running out. We created this crisis, which means we still have the power to change its course. Act before it's too late.

note: Facebook post with an embedded NOAA temperature-departures bar chart + specific data claim (1.09C above avg, second-warmest June in 177-year record). Specific + checkable -> CLAIM. Tests infographic/chart type and a NOAA visible citation.

expected.claim_text: The June 2026 global surface temperature was 1.09°C (1.96°F) above average—the second warmest June in the 177-year record. This value was 0.09°C (0.16°F) below the 2024 record and only 0.02°C (0.04°F) above June 2023, which currently ranks as the third warmest June on record. The 10 warmest Junes on record have all occurred since 2015, with each of the last eight years (2019–2026) ranking among them.

note: Facebook (verified Yale program). Fire-photo card with an overlay forecast headline; body has specific WMO projections. FORECAST claim -> tests the forecast reframe. image_type=infographic (text-over-photo card).

expected.claim_text: There is a 75% chance the average global temperature between 2026 and 2030 will exceed 2.7°F (the 1.5°C Paris threshold) above pre-industrial levels, according to new projections from the World Meteorological Organization and the U.K. Meteorological Office. The projections are based on 200 simulation runs across 13 different climate models. The WMO report also finds a 91% chance that at least one of the next five years will individually cross that threshold, and an 86% chance that at least one year will break the global heat record set in 2024. The Arctic is projected to warm 3.5 times faster than the global average. The Amazon faces increasing drought and wildfire risk. Scientists emphasize that the 1.5°C threshold is not a single tipping point. Every additional tenth of a degree carries measurable consequences: more extreme heat events, intensified wildfires, stronger storms, and greater strain on food systems. Despite these risks, research from YPCCC finds that only 47% of people in frontline communities, those most exposed to climate impacts, report hearing about global warming in the media at least once a month.

note: X/Twitter (verified @US_Stormwatch). Specific claim (El Nino ~7 weeks ahead, RONI peak 3.6C) + an SST anomaly map from tropicaltidbits. Tests exact @handle transcription, high-engagement X counts (likes/reposts/replies), infographic map, visible citation.

expected.claim_text: The 2026-27 El Niño is simply astonishing. Tropical Pacific waters are running nearly 7 weeks ahead of where they've ever been at this point in an El Niño cycle in modern history. Models now put the peak strength at 3.6°C on the Relative Oceanic Niño Index (RONI).

note: X/Twitter (verified @forallcurious). REAL PHOTOS of beached whales that DEPICT the claimed event -> real_photo + depicts_claim=yes = the precision-save path (a real on-the-ground photo clears the red flag). Specific event (145 carcasses, US West Coast, 2026) -> CLAIM.

expected.claim_text: Scientists just declared "Catastrophic mortality event" over whale deaths. Over 145 whale carcasses washed ashore on the U.S. West Coast in 2026, signaling a devastating population collapse driven by climate change and starvation.

note: X/Twitter. Our World in Data chart whose TITLE states the claim (cherry blossoms earlier due to warmer springs, 1200-yr Kyoto series). Somewhat image-carried (soft tweet framing, claim in the chart) + self-cited (OWID + named papers). Specific/checkable -> CLAIM. Author header cropped -> handle null.

expected.claim_text: Cherry trees are blossoming earlier due to warmer spring temperatures

note: X/Twitter (@PoyetPatrice). CONTRARIAN/skeptic post accusing a climate chart of being 'fake graphs' and citing the original paper figure (stops 2007). LOW engagement (2 likes) -> should NOT red-flag. Borderline claim/opinion: labelled CLAIM for the checkable assertion about the source data. Tests skeptic + low-reach.

expected.claim_text: "Simon Kuestenmacher curate #maps and #data that explain how the #world works". Which means he produces fake graphs. Here's one the original graphs from the paper (that stops in 2007 - date of publication)

note: LinkedIn. Framing statement (climate = next public-health challenge) over a scenic river/mountains photo UNRELATED to the claim. Tests real_photo + depicts_claim=unrelated => NO precision save (image doesn't depict the claim). Framing -> OPINION. No engagement counts shown -> all null.

expected.claim_text: Climate Change Is No Longer Just an Environmental Issue. It Is Becoming the Next Great Public Health Challenge.

note: LinkedIn article share (weather-warning map cover). Title explicitly 'A personal reflection' -> essay/OPINION, no specific checkable event. Tests reflection/essay=opinion + an infographic weather map. Platform inferred from the article-card + pronoun format (judgment call).

expected.claim_text: When the Sky Turned Orange: A personal reflection on climate, responsibility, and the choices we make for future generations

note: Standalone promotional infographic (a course ad, not a climate claim). Readable text but NO checkable climate assertion -> OPINION (robustness case: has_readable_text true, nothing to corroborate). No platform chrome -> platform null. NB: the enroll URL (vacancybridge.com) impersonating UN University is itself a human red flag, out of the classifier's scope.

expected.claim_text: FREE Climate Change Training Programme for Students Worldwide 2026 — United Nations University. 100% online, free to join, UN certificate.

note: Bare press photo of a climate-justice rally (no platform chrome). Readable text is protest-banner ADVOCACY ('unite for food, land, and climate justice', 'End fossil fuels') -> OPINION, not a checkable event. Tests real_photo + readable advocacy signs + null platform/engagement.

expected.claim_text: Rural peoples unite for food, land, and climate justice

note: Article header: fire photo with an overlaid RHETORICAL QUESTION headline ('Will AI Destroy Us With Carbon Emissions?'). A question asserts nothing checkable -> OPINION (rhetorical_question). Tests text-over-photo banner + question-not-claim. No platform/engagement.

expected.claim_text: Forget Taking Over the World: Will AI Destroy Us With Carbon Emissions?

note: Detailed educational infographic (causes/impacts/solutions). Leads with a general scientific claim ('global warming is the long-term increase in Earth's average temperature, mainly caused by human activities') -> a checkable causal assertion -> CLAIM (general/educational, borderline). Tests a dense infographic + a general-science claim.

expected.claim_text: Global warming is the long-term increase in Earth's average temperature, mainly caused by human activities.

note: SCREENSHOT of a Google image/search result showing an NYT article (fills the 'screenshot of another site' gap) — Google Lens icon + '- The New York Times' suffix + embedded real flood photo. News headline about a specific real event -> CLAIM. visible_citation = The New York Times. NB: nytimes.com is now in citation_domains, but the image shows the NAME not the domain, so it isn't auto-credited (name->domain resolution is future work).

expected.claim_text: What We Know About the Climate Connection to the European Floods
